profile.name: Bean Dong
profile.kicker: Factory Test Software / StationSW / SFD / Atlas2 / Team Delivery.
profile.title:
Consumer products reveal the result.
Manufacturing and R&D hide the reasons.

Behind every polished product is more than brand story.
There is engineering pressure, human cost, efficiency, and countless negotiations.

My work is not always glamorous.
It lives in the muddiest part of production — where technology, process, people, and cost meet.
That is where I learned to see the greatness of a product from the inside out.
profile.summary: I started as an app developer and grew into factory test software ownership, building station workflows, supporting production readiness, debugging complex issues, and helping teams deliver stable test systems across multiple Apple CM projects.

metrics.title: Years in factory test software
metrics.label: NPI, dry run, build validation, issue debug, and mass-production support across hardware/software test workflows.

metrics.title: CM project experience
metrics.label: Public product coverage includes iMac Pro, Intel MacBook Pro / Air programs, 15-inch MacBook Pro, Pro Display XDR, M-series MacBook Pro / Air programs, Apple TV programs, and current NPI work.

metrics.title: Platform generations
metrics.label: Hands-on experience across Atlas1, Atlas2-Matchbox, and Atlas2-Schooner migration, structure, compatibility, and station workflow work.

metrics.title: Engineering skill surfaces
metrics.label: StationSW, QT development, SFD-Diags support, automation scripting, and production-readiness tooling.

metrics.title: Code and scripting languages
metrics.label: Lua, Shell, Python, Objective-C, plus project-side QT / station software development experience.

metrics.title: Delivery roles
metrics.label: Developer, QT owner, CMSW / ShippingSetting owner, and SW tech leader supporting team-level delivery.

career.short: My career started in Hangzhou as an iOS app developer, where I helped build two applications from 0 to 1 and supported multiple App Store release iterations. I later joined QSMC and moved from pure software development into factory test software. The XDR Display Pro project became a major turning point: under high pressure and limited resources, I began to design test stations and OVL structure independently. After that, I supported multiple Mac projects, participated in Atlas1 to Atlas2 / Matchbox architecture transition, and started mentoring new engineers through QT development, dry run support, code review, and team delivery. At WT / Luxshare, I returned to Apple CM project work with broader StationSW responsibility, focusing on factory test software, automation tools, workflow optimization, production readiness, and team-level delivery.
story: My career started in Hangzhou as an iOS app developer, where I helped build two applications from 0 to 1 and supported multiple App Store release iterations.

Later at QSMC, I moved from pure software development into factory test software. The Pro Display XDR project became a turning point — I began to design test stations and OVL structure independently under pressure and limited resources.

At WT / Luxshare, I returned to Apple CM project work with broader StationSW responsibility: factory test software, automation tools, workflow optimization, production readiness, and team-level delivery.

work.overview: My work sits between software development and real factory execution. I build and maintain station software, and lead the team in building and maintaining stable test workflows across NPI and mass-production phases. I also support dry runs, build validation, production issue debugging, and cross-functional coordination.
work.currentRole: Owning StationSW delivery across current and established programs. From bring-up to production readiness.
work.overlapNote: Projects are not linear. StationSW, SFD, Diags, and SPL move together across bring-up, migration, release readiness, and MP support.

highlights: Current major project ownership across multiple stations.
Own CMSW work across multiple SMT and FATP station workstreams.
Support calibration, I/O, QT, and related StationSW workstreams.
Support station software, test flow development, issue debug, and production readiness.
Coordinate with OPS, HWTE, EE, Diags, SFD, SPL, and other function teams.
Focus on StationSW, Diags, and SPL stability, workflow optimization, and team delivery.
timeline.details: Expanding from single-station development to broader StationSW ownership across bring-up, workflow optimization, production readiness, and team delivery.

highlights: Owned ShippingSetting-related work for the MacBook Air project.
Trusted with customer-side access to maintain ShippingSetting and related StationSW code under a new collaboration model.
Supported SFD activities and cross-functional issue analysis.
Worked across production, validation, and release support phases.
Maintained stable factory software delivery over a long project lifecycle.
Supported overlapping projects through team coverage and parallel ownership.
timeline.details: Long-cycle ownership across ShippingSetting, production stability, release support, and cross-functional issue analysis.

timeline.role: QT Atlas1 to Atlas2 Migration Dev
highlights: Supported Atlas1 to Atlas2 migration for the AppleTV project.
Worked on architecture transfer and compatibility validation.
Helped align legacy test flow with Atlas2 structure.
Supported station readiness, issue debug, and build validation.
Strengthened experience in platform migration and test flow refactoring.
timeline.details: Migrating legacy test flows from Atlas1 to Atlas2 through architecture transfer, compatibility validation, and station readiness.

highlights: Expanded project experience beyond the Mac product line.
Supported AppleTV QT development and factory test validation.
Adapted StationSW experience to a different product category.
Worked with cross-functional teams during build support.
Strengthened ability to support multiple product families.
timeline.details: Extending StationSW experience beyond Mac into a new product structure and factory validation flow.

timeline.scope: QT Ownership / Team Development / Architecture Transition
highlights: Supported early M-series MacBook Pro project delivery.
Took ownership of QT flow, schedule, dry run, and station readiness.
Participated in Atlas1 to Atlas2 / Matchbox architecture transition.
Built reusable methods and common logic for team development.
Started training new team members and moving toward team-level delivery.
timeline.details: Moving from individual QT ownership toward reusable architecture, code review, mentoring, and team-level delivery.

timeline.scope: QT Ownership / New Station Development / OVL Structure
highlights: First major breakthrough as CMSW / QT owner.
Independently handled new test station development and project support.
Designed and optimized OVL structure for flexible flash version builds.
Supported DOE / FA needs through configurable software flow.
Transitioned from modifying existing code to owning system delivery.
timeline.details: A breakthrough from modifying existing code to independently designing test stations, OVL structure, and reusable delivery tools.

timeline.scope: QT Development / Dry Run / Build Support
highlights: Supported QT development for the MacBook Pro project.
Gained hands-on experience with dry run, build support, and test flow validation.
Improved understanding of fixture, station, and production test interactions.
Started handling common test issues more independently.
Built stronger foundation in Mac factory test software.
timeline.details: Building practical factory test foundations across station software, fixtures, dry runs, test flow, and production validation.

timeline.scope: Mac Factory Test Software / Early Build Support
highlights: First Apple CM project experience.
Started working on Mac-related factory test software.
Learned basic StationSW workflow, build support, and production test process.
Began transitioning from app development to factory test software.
Built early understanding of hardware-related software validation.
timeline.details: The starting point of my factory test software career and the transition into production software and hardware-related validation.

timeline.scope: App Development / App Store Release / Product Iteration
highlights: Started professional software career as an iOS developer.
Participated in building two commercial apps from 0 to 1.
Supported around a dozen App Store version updates.
Improved application experience based on user feedback and business requirements.
Built early software development habits through real product iteration.
timeline.details: Building commercial iOS products from zero to release, then improving them through real user feedback and repeated App Store iterations.

profile: 8+ years experience in NPI and mass-production HW/SW test engineering across SMT and FATP for Apple Mac, iMac, AppleTV, XDR and M-series products.
Lead CM StationSW team and own station development, data collection, qualification, debug/FA and new version release, collaborating with vendors and cross-functional teams for new ID design products.

Highly recognized by customers and counterparts for SW solutions, troubleshooting skills and testing management, with the ability to quickly learn new technologies and handle multi-task problem-solving and communication effectively in high-intensity environments.
Bilingual engineer capable of managing test readiness and driving station deliverables from prototype to MP.

experience.company: Shanghai Wingtech / Luxshare Technology Co., Ltd.
products: May 2023 - Aug. 2023 · AppleTV II · Atlas1 Transfer Atlas2 Dev
Oct. 2021 - Jun. 2024 · MacBook Air · ShippingSetting Owner & SFD Support
Jun. 2025 - Recent · Current NPI Program · Multi-station StationSW Owner
responsibilities: Lead the SW Tech team responsible for SFD, Diags and StationSW development, resource planning and customer requirement communication.
Collaborate closely with customer engineering through approved source-control workflows to co-develop new station features and coverage, and resolve project issues.
Independently owned and managed ShippingSetting station development during the Mac project.
Train and mentor junior engineers, reviewing code, defining technical standards and ensuring consistent readiness, data collection and station performance across all builds.

products: Jun. 2017 - Dec. 2017 · iMac Pro · Mac1 Dev
Dec. 2017 - Jul. 2018 · 15-inch MacBook Pro · QT Dev
Aug. 2018 - Oct. 2019 · XDR Display Pro · QT Owner
Oct. 2019 - Apr. 2021 · M-series MacBook Pro · QT Owner
responsibilities: Provided technical support and manufacturing solutions for Apple test stations.
Delivered product verification design, code coverage development, data analysis and engineering documentation.
Collaborated with customer engineers on site to build and validate test coverage for new product features.
Managed project tasks, assigned work to team members and coordinated with cross-functional departments.

Developed and upgraded two commercial iOS applications released on the App Store (Yanchuan Education and ShiZunCiHang), improving user experience and maintaining the application codebase based on user feedback and new business requirements.

skills.name: Technical Skills
items: Proficient in Atlas2 development; familiar with Schooner, Matchbox and Burgundy structure.
Skilled in Linux shell script, Lua and Rush (Atlas1); familiar with Objective-C.
Medium Computer Science knowledge and computer hardware setting.

resume.education: 2011-2015 · Shandong Agricultural University · Electronic Information Science and Technology · Bachelor of Engineering.

life.heroSubtitle: A life shaped by curiosity, creation, games, motion, and the things I genuinely enjoy.

life.galleryTitle: A few things that keep life interesting.
life.statementBody: Some things are simply interest-driven. A drawing, a landscape, a finished build, or a hard game may not belong on a resume, but they still shape how life feels.
life.finaleBody: Work builds capability. Life keeps curiosity alive.

contact.cardBody: Professional: factory test software, NPI delivery, StationSW ownership, and cross-functional engineering. Custom PC services: premium graphics card sourcing from overseas markets, DIY build consulting, and end-to-end high-end PC assembly in Shanghai.
